title: The Psychology of Phone Distraction
description: Why the phone wins when focus gets uncomfortable.

clips.narration: Your hand moves toward the phone before you decide to.
The laptop is open. The work is right there. Something still feels heavy.
You tell yourself one quick check. The screen becomes the whole room.
Every idle moment gets filled. Waiting becomes scrolling.
Everyone else looks busy. Your phone becomes a shield.
Discomfort arrives. The phone offers instant relief.
At night the loop gets louder. Sleep waits while the feed does not.
The couch, the remote, the scroll. One continuous dodge.
Focus was the goal. The phone traded it for a hit of ease.
Distraction is not weakness. It is your nervous system reaching for relief. Put the phone down for one breath, then one line of work.
